km.Devon, England: ដិវ៉ុន, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubyn是Guillaume de Saint-Aubyn II的兒子，於大約1130年出生在英國戴文。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubyn是Guillaume de Saint-Aubyn II的儿子，于大约1130年出生在英国戴文。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Mauger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and Channel Islands (Guernsey and Jersey): from the Old French (Norman) personal name Maug(i)er, Malg(i)er, from ancient Germanic Madalgar(i), Malger, composed of the elements madal ‘council, meeting’ and gar(i) ‘spear’, or from Amalgar(i), with the first element amal ‘capable, brave, eager’. Compare Major , Monger , and Munger .
Probably also an altered form of German   Mager  .

variants.name: Maugeri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Ignazio, Salvatore, Angelo, Camillo, Giuseppe, Orazio, Pietro.
Italian (southern):
variant of Malgeri, from the ancient Germanic personal name Madalgari or Amalgari (see Mauger ).

variants.name: Major
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Malg(i)er, Maug(i)er, an Old French form of ancient Germanic Madalgar(i) (see Mauger ).
Hungarian: from a shortened form of majorosgazda (see Majoros ) or a derivative of German Meyer .
Polish, Czech, and Slovak: from the military rank major (derived from Latin maior ‘greater’), a word related to English mayor and the German surname Meyer .

variants.name: Mangieri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Mario, Angelo, Arsenio, Mafalda, Orazio, Rocco, Silvio.
Italian: variant of Malgeri, from the ancient Germanic personal name Madalgari or Amalgari (see Mauger ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: St
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Aubyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Andre, Normand, Armand, Cecile, Emile, Henri, Monique, Adelard, Aime, Alcid, Aldege.

French: from the personal name Aubin (from Latin Albinus, a derivative of albus ‘white’). This was the name of several minor early Christian saints, including a famous bishop of Angers (died c. 554). At an early date, this name became confused with the ancient Germanic personal name Albuin (see Albin ). Compare Obey and Obin .
English: variant of   Albin  .

variants.name: Albin
meanings: English, French, and German: from the personal name Albin (from Latin Albinus, a derivative of albus ‘white’). In England this is generally a variant of Alban . The usual spelling of the French name is Aubin . The personal name was especially popular in Austria, Lombardy, and Savoy, where it absorbed the ancient Germanic name Albuin (which is composed of the elements alb ‘elf’ + win ‘friend’; compare Alvin ). This was the name of the Lombard leader (died 572) who made himself king of northern Italy, and also of various Christian saints, including a bishop of Brixen (Bressanone) in South Tyrol.
Galician (Albín): habitational name from any of the four places called Albín in Galicia (Spain), from Latin (villa) Albini.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubyn was born about 1130, in Devon, England as the son of Guillaume de Saint-Aubyn II. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubyn est né environ 1130, à Devon, Angleterre, fils de Guillaume de Saint-Aubyn II. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubyn wurde um 1130 in Devon, England, als Sohn von Guillaume de Saint-Aubyn II geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubyn è nato il circa 1130 nel Devon, England come figlio di Guillaume de Saint-Aubyn II. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubynは約1130年にイギリスデボンでGuillaume de Saint-Aubyn IIの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubyn(은)는 대략 1130년 영국 데폰에서 Guillaume de Saint-Aubyn II의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubyn nasceu aproximadamente 1130, em Devon, Inglaterra filho de Guillaume de Saint-Aubyn II. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubyn родился около 1130 г. в местности девон, Англия. Его отца звали Guillaume de Saint-Aubyn II. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mauger de St. Aubyn nació aproximadamente 1130, en Devon, Inglaterra como hijo de Guillaume de Saint-Aubyn II. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
